Elementwise.issues: All 59 mapped tests fail. Benchmark log reports 0 qualified despite per-operator bench=passed entries — benchmark results are present in operator metadata but not reflected in the log summary, suggesting a reporting pipeline disconnect. 11 ops have no bench data at all.
Elementwise.evaluation: Largest category with near-total test failure and a benchmark reporting anomaly. Investigate why bench log shows 0 qualified when individual ops show bench=passed — likely a log aggregation bug. Prioritize fixing the shared test harness for binary_arith and unary_math groups first since they cover the most ops.

Reduce.issues: All 20 ops fail tests. Same benchmark log discrepancy as Elementwise — all ops show bench=passed individually but log reports 0 qualified. Full test and bench coverage exists, making this a high-priority fix.
Reduce.evaluation: 100% test failure rate with complete operator and benchmark coverage means failures are likely systemic rather than op-specific. Run a single reduce op in isolation to confirm whether the issue is in the test framework or the ops themselves, then fix the benchmark aggregation reporting.

Norm.issues: All 9 implemented ops fail tests. qk_norm is unimplemented with no test or bench. Same benchmark log discrepancy — 9 ops show bench=passed individually but log reports 0 qualified.
Norm.evaluation: Well-covered category blocked by the same systemic test failure. qk_norm should be prioritized for implementation given its relevance to attention mechanisms. Resolve the global test failure root cause to unblock this category quickly.

Conv & Pooling.issues: Zero implementation across all 16 ops. No tests, no benchmarks. Entire category is unstarted.
Conv & Pooling.evaluation: This category is a blank slate and likely deprioritized given TileOPs' LLM focus. If conv support is on the roadmap, start with conv2d and max_pool2d as the most commonly needed ops. Otherwise, formally mark this category as out-of-scope to keep metrics clean.

GEMM.issues: Only 9 of 19 ops implemented. 3 ops fail tests; 7 implemented ops have no test mapping. Lowbit GEMM variants (w4a16, w8a8, fp4, sparse) are entirely unimplemented. Benchmark log shows 0 qualified despite 11 ops showing bench=passed individually.
GEMM.evaluation: Core GEMM ops exist but test coverage is thin and lowbit variants are missing entirely. Given LLM inference workloads, w4a16 and w8a8 should be high-priority implementations. Add test mappings for the 7 implemented ops that currently show test=missing.

Quantize.issues: Only fp8_per_tensor is implemented; its test fails and its benchmark fails. fp8_per_block benchmark also fails despite no implementation. 8 of 10 ops are entirely unimplemented with no test or bench coverage.
Quantize.evaluation: Critical gap for an LLM operator library — quantization is foundational for inference efficiency. The fp8_per_tensor benchmark failure needs immediate investigation. Prioritize implementing int8_per_tensor and int8_per_channel as the most broadly applicable quantization ops.

Sampling.issues: Only chain_speculative_sampling is implemented; both its test and benchmark fail. All other 6 ops are unimplemented with no coverage.
Sampling.evaluation: Sampling is nearly entirely unimplemented. top_k and top_p are the most critical ops for LLM decoding and should be implemented first. Fix the chain_speculative_sampling test and benchmark failures to validate the existing implementation before expanding.

Flash Attention.issues: 8 of 16 ops implemented, all 8 fail tests. MLA variants (prefill fwd/bwd, decode paged), varlen decode, chunked prefill, and both DSA/NSA decode ops are unimplemented. Benchmark log shows 0 qualified despite 8 ops showing bench=passed individually.
Flash Attention.evaluation: Flash Attention has solid implementation breadth for a high-priority category but is blocked by the systemic test failure. MLA decode is implemented and should be a quick win once tests are fixed. Prioritize flash_prefill_varlen_bwd and mla_prefill_fwd as the next implementation targets.

MoE.issues: Zero implementations across all 6 ops. permute_align has a test mapping that fails despite impl=N, which is a data integrity issue. Only 1 benchmark exists (permute_align bench=passed) but log reports 0 qualified.
MoE.evaluation: MoE is unimplemented but critical for modern LLM architectures like DeepSeek and Qwen. The permute_align test=failed with impl=N is a metadata inconsistency that should be corrected. Start implementation with fused_moe_deepseek given its industry relevance.

Linear Attention.issues: 4 of 8 ops implemented; all 6 mapped tests fail. gla_chunkwise and gla_recurrence have test mappings and benchmarks but impl=N — metadata inconsistency. retnet variants are fully unimplemented. Benchmark log shows 0 qualified despite 6 ops showing bench=passed.
Linear Attention.evaluation: DeltaNet variants are implemented but blocked by systemic test failures. The gla impl=N with test=failed and bench=passed is a metadata conflict that needs resolution — either the implementation exists and impl flag is wrong, or the test/bench mappings are stale. Clarify gla status and implement retnet_chunkwise next.

SSM.issues: Both mamba1 and mamba2 are unimplemented with no test or benchmark coverage.
SSM.evaluation: SSM category is entirely unstarted. Mamba2 should be prioritized over Mamba1 given its broader adoption in recent architectures. No blocking issues beyond missing implementation.

overall.summary: TileOPs has meaningful implementation breadth — 122 of 186 ops implemented — but zero functional validation: all 108 mapped tests fail across every category, suggesting a systemic issue in the test harness rather than widespread operator bugs. Benchmark data exists at the operator level for ~116 ops but the aggregation pipeline reports 0 qualified across all categories, indicating a second systemic failure in the reporting layer. Critical LLM inference categories like Quantize, Sampling, and MoE are severely underimplemented.
recommendations: Diagnose the systemic test failure immediately by running a single well-understood op (e.g., add or sum) in isolation — if it passes standalone but fails in the CI harness, the issue is in test infrastructure (XML parsing, environment setup, or mapping config) rather than operator correctness.

Fix the benchmark log aggregation pipeline — individual ops correctly report bench=passed but the category-level log shows 0 qualified everywhere, meaning benchmark CI results are not being collected or parsed correctly and are masking real performance data.

Prioritize implementing Quantize (int8_per_tensor, int8_per_channel) and Sampling (top_k, top_p) as the next development targets, since these are foundational for LLM inference and currently have near-zero coverage.
